skill.name: brand-guide
skill.description: Create and maintain brand style guides that document colors, typography, imagery, voice, responsive behavior, and WordPress page workflows.
skill.summary: Build practical brand guides from existing WordPress theme details or from a structured brand brief.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak-cryptography and most command-execution alerts are false positives from color values, CSS samples, Markdown code fences, and documentation. The real risks are moderate: the skill includes a Python script that reads theme files and writes an output file, plus documented shell and Docker examples that users may run locally. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, network callback code, or prompt injection attempt was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Theme File Access and Output Writes
medium_findings.description: The extraction script accepts a user supplied theme path, reads common WordPress theme files, and can write extracted brand data to a user supplied output path. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it gives the skill filesystem reach over selected local project files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly uses the supplied path to read theme files and optionally writes output. The behavior is intentional and bounded to named files, so this is a moderate operational risk rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands for WordPress Publishing
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Python and Docker command examples, including shell command substitution for a WordPress page body. The commands support the documented workflow, but users should review files before running the examples in a shell.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and include command substitution, but the reviewed substitution reads a local HTML file and does not show attacker controlled interpolation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the reviewed locations contain brand color hex values, RGB values, typography sizes, CSS examples, and regular expression parsing. No cryptographic algorithm use was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visibly color and document parsing data, not cryptographic API usage. Multiple high-severity static alerts are therefore false positives.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to a Google Fonts specimen page in a Markdown example. No code performs a network request or sends local data to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears as plain documentation under the primary font section. There is no fetch, request library, or network-capable script associated with it.

low_findings.title: Absolute Local Paths in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill references absolute local paths such as Claude skill directories and a CSR theme directory. These references are documentation and examples, but they can expose environment assumptions and should be generalized before publication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are directly present in documentation. They are not executed by code, so the risk is information exposure and portability rather than active file theft.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Trigger Command Alerts
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts in the example brand guide are caused by Markdown inline code and fenced CSS or HTML snippets. Reviewed examples describe visual styles and assets rather than executable shell logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported regions are Markdown examples for color swatches, CSS, and HTML components. They do not define executable commands or a code execution path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Reads from User Supplied Theme Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The Python script reads files under the requested theme path to extract colors and fonts. This is expected, but users should run it only on trusted project directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script performs direct local file reads from the provided path. The reviewed logic limits reads to known theme filenames and does not transmit results.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The WordPress publishing example uses command substitution to pass local HTML into wp-cli. This should be treated as a reviewed local command, not an automated action.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell pattern is real, but its reviewed usage is a local cat command for documented WordPress content publishing.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose brand consistency when colors, fonts, image rules, and voice guidance live in scattered files. This skill turns brand details into a practical style guide for design, content, and WordPress implementation work.
actual_capabilities: Documents color palettes with hex values, RGB values, and usage guidance.
Defines typography rules for headings, body text, labels, and responsive sizes.
Captures imagery direction, logo usage notes, aspect ratios, and UI component guidance.
Provides a Python script that extracts colors and fonts from common WordPress theme files.
Outlines workflows for creating a brand guide from an existing theme or a new brand brief.
Includes a complete CSR Real Estate example guide for reference structure and tone.
limitations: The repository includes one extraction script; referenced guide generation utilities are not present in the file tree.
The extraction script reads only selected WordPress theme filenames and does not parse every asset type.
Brand voice, imagery rules, and strategic positioning still require human review.
WordPress publishing examples require a compatible local Docker and wp-cli setup.

use_cases.title: Document an Existing WordPress Brand
use_cases.description: Extract theme colors and fonts, then turn them into a readable guide for future site updates.

use_cases.title: Create a Client Style Guide
use_cases.description: Convert approved brand decisions into clear rules for colors, type, imagery, components, and tone.

use_cases.title: Align Content and Design Teams
use_cases.description: Give writers and designers one reference for voice, visual treatment, responsive behavior, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new brand brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand guide outline for this brand: [brand name]. Include colors, typography, imagery, voice, and responsive notes.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Theme Details
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for an existing WordPress theme.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this WordPress theme directory and summarize the brand colors, fonts, logo assets, and reusable UI patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Expand Design Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving incomplete guidelines.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these brand notes into detailed guidance for color usage, typography hierarchy, image treatments, buttons, cards, and forms.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Implementation Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for design and development teams.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-ready brand guide for a WordPress page, including responsive rules, component behavior, voice examples, and review notes.

output_examples.input: A real estate brand needs consistent colors, typography, and page behavior documented.
output: A color palette section with names, hex values, RGB values, and approved usage.
A typography section covering headline, body, label, and caption styles.
Responsive guidance for navigation, hero sections, portfolio grids, and footer layout.

output_examples.input: A WordPress theme has existing CSS and PHP files but no formal brand guide.
output: A summary of detected colors and fonts from selected theme files.
A draft guide structure that separates extracted facts from items needing human confirmation.
A list of implementation notes for publishing the guide as a WordPress page.

output_examples.input: A marketing team needs voice and imagery rules for future pages.
output: Voice attributes with plain-language writing examples.
Imagery rules for subject matter, treatment, and aspect ratios.
Do and do-not guidance for maintaining brand consistency.

best_practices: Review extracted colors and fonts before treating them as approved brand rules.
Separate factual implementation details from strategic guidance that needs stakeholder approval.
Test any WordPress publishing command in a local or staging environment before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not run the extraction script on directories that contain unrelated sensitive files.
Do not publish generated brand language without human review from the brand owner.
Do not assume referenced local paths exist in every Claude, Codex, or Claude Code environment.

faq.question: Can this skill extract brand data from an existing site?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python script that reads selected WordPress theme files and extracts colors and fonts.

faq.question: Does it create a complete brand strategy?
faq.answer: No. It organizes and documents brand rules, but positioning and messaging decisions need human review.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No network request code was found. One example links to a Google Fonts specimen page for documentation.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked medium?
faq.answer: The skill can guide local script execution and file reads. Review paths and commands before running them.

faq.question: Are all referenced helper scripts included?
faq.answer: No. The file tree includes extract-brand.py, while some documented helper scripts are not present.
